rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 12, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ROOSEVELT JOHNSON Motion For Assignment Order. Continued to June 21 to give the opposing party an opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to department 302 no later than 4pm June 13 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. Failure to do so will result in granting the motion. The court received courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 12, 2012, Line 18. CROSS COMPLAINANT FRONT STREET BUILDING, LLC Motion To Compel The Deposition And Production Of Documents Of Dinesh Maniar. OFF calendar per request of moving party. The motion has been re-noticed for July 10, 2012. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PRABHABEN PATEL VS. CARL F. WILLGEROTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 12, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied in its entirety. There are triable issues regarding (1) the continued validity of the first promissory note, (2) the effect, if any, of the second promissory note, and (3) whether Plaintiff was supposed to submit the UCC-1 financial statement to escrow in order to be paid on the first promissory note. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAD MASSIS VS. NILESH MANILAL SHAH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order That Req.'S For Admissions, Set No. One, To Julinar Msalam, Be Deemed Admitted And Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 12, 2012, LINE 1, PLAINTIFF JAD MASSIS' MOTION FOR ORDER THAT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET NO. ONE, TO JULINAR MSALAM, BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 12, 2012, LINE 2, PLANTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. To be heard at 9:30 am in Department 302 on Law and Motion line 19. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. POLENG LOUNGE, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 12, 2012, Line 19. Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Denied. Judgment Debtor has failed to show that a statutory exemption applies. Although the address on the notice of levy may have been incorrect, failure to serve the writ of execution, notice of levy, or list of exemptions does not affect the validity of the execution lien created by the levy. The $3105.26 currently being held by the Sheriff shall be released to the Judgment Creditor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: 255 STEINER LLC et al VS. JEROME J GHIGLIOTTI JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr., A Professional Corporation And Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr.'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Per Ex Parte Order Filed May-4-2012)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 12, 2012, Line 22. Defendants Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr., A Professional Corporation And Jerome J. Ghigliotti, Jr.'S Motion For Summary Judgment. Denied. The motion does not address all the allegations of malpractice. In particular, the motion does not address at lines 26 and 27 of page 2 of the second amended complaint that defendants committed malpractice by including Plaintiff JAMES BROUSSARD as a named plaintiff in the action, in lieu of solely Plaintiff 255 Steiner, LLC, the owner of 255 Steiner Street." Moreover, assuming without deciding that a three day notice was proper, there are triable issues whether inclusion of a cure period in the notice and whether seeking appellate review rather than serving a new notice fell below the standard of care. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRET CORNELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel The Deposition Of Deft George Gascon; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 12, 2012, Line 21. PLAINTIFF BRET CORNELL Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Deft George Gascon. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Rosenquist as follows: Granted. The scope of the deposition is limited to: (a) the witness' personal knowledge concerning the facts and circumstances of the plaintiff's case and his input or actions; (b) the issuance of the termination memorandum and termination letter, and any conversations or communications in connection with the termination; (c) policies and procedures only with respect to the facts of the plaintiff's case and with respect to the plaintiff. Plaintiff may not conduct a general inquiry into administrative or HR policies with respect to third persons or hypothetical situations; (d) Plaintiff may not depose the witness concerning the records or history of the other officers involved in the case within the scope of a Pitchess inquiry. This is not a ruling on a Pitchess request but a subject for a later motion or decision by the court. However, if there were conversations between Chief Gascón and any other officers concerning the plaintiff or the facts of the case, that inquiry may be pursued; and (e) The deposition shall not exceed 4 hours. Prior to the hearing, the parties shall meet and confer on a suitable protective order and present their results at the hearing. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery motion. If any party wishes a court reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONNA LANE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Amend Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 12, 2012, Line 11. PLAINTIFF DONNA LANE Motion To Amend Complaint. Granted. The trial continued to December 10, 2012. All discovery deadlines are triggered off the new trial date. The August 7, 2012 hearing is off calendar. Defendant may file a new motion for summary judgment. With the trial continuance, defendant suffers no prejudice by the granting of the motion and thus the strong policy of liberally granting amendments controls. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST CHIN VS. MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Ofm Otion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JUNE 12, 2012, LINE 10, PLAINTIFF ERNEST CHIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)
